subject: Computer Networking
title: How Data Travels Through Cables
description: A short lesson to help you apply How Data Travels Through Cables.

hook.text: Your message crosses an ocean in milliseconds. It travels as light through glass thinner than a hair.

buildup.text: Fiber optic cables use pulses of light to carry data. Copper cables use electrical signals instead.

discovery.text: Light in fiber is faster and carries more data. That's why the internet's backbone is fiber, not copper.

twist.text: Undersea cables carry 99% of intercontinental traffic. Satellites handle only a tiny fraction.

climax.text: A single modern undersea cable can carry hundreds of terabits per second across the Atlantic.

punchline.text: The internet's real highway is glass under the ocean.

quiz.question: How does most intercontinental internet traffic travel?
